title: S. elongatus stock revival
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"eu9t4","text":"Stocks are more permanent methods of storing bacteria than plates. These stocks can be recovered by pouring them into small volumes of BG-11 and growing under low light levels. As the culture grows, it can be passaged into higher volumes and appropriate antibiotics can be added.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b9ep4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Add your stock to 2 mL BG-11 medium in a test tube and grow it overnight. Do not add any antibiotics
Transfer this to a 50 mL flask and make up the culture to 10 mL in volume.
Once it reaches a lime green colour (#32CD32), add a fresh medium to make the culture up to 50 mL culture in a 150 mL
Further, make this culture up to 100 mL in a 250 mL flask once it returns to lime green.
creator: Akashdutta
